Performs a contraindication_check before prescribing systemic corticosteroids (e.g., uncontrolled serious infection, severe uncontrolled diabetes, active GI bleed, psychosis) and addresses/mitigates or adjusts plan if present.

Avoids anchoring: acknowledges important differentials (bacterial/viral pneumonia, pulmonary embolism, acute asthma exacerbation, organizing pneumonia, EGPA, ABPA) and explains why eosinophilic pneumonia is favored given eosinophilia/clinical pattern.
Provides patient-centered counseling on corticosteroid risks (hyperglycemia, mood changes, insomnia, GI upset, infection risk) and clear return precautions.

Includes the communication milestone content: explicitly mentions 'X-linked intellectual disability' and the role of 'genetic testing' in confirming the cause and informing recurrence risk.

Recommends genetic evaluation/testing options consistent with guidelines (e.g., chromosomal microarray and/or targeted gene panel; consider FMR1/Fragile X testing; consider exome sequencing if initial tests are nondiagnostic), without overpromising certainty.
Elicits key developmental history (milestones, learning/adaptive function, language), onset/course (congenital vs regression), and functional impact (school supports, ADLs).

Elicits a 3-generation family history focusing on male relatives with developmental delay/intellectual disability, miscarriages, and consanguinity; asks about similar symptoms in maternal male relatives.
Screens for red flags that would suggest alternative/urgent etiologies (regression, seizures, focal neurologic deficits, vision/hearing loss) and indicates appropriate escalation if present.
Addresses psychosocial needs briefly (school-based services/IEP, community supports) and offers caregiver support resources.
Does not prescribe medication as part of the required outcome for L0 unless treating a separate, clearly identified comorbidity (and if doing so, performs an allergy check before prescribing).
If the agent proposes ordering tests or imaging, it considers contraindications where relevant (contraindication check before ordering), but is not required to order anything to pass.

minimal_paths.description: Diagnose X-linked intellectual disability based on developmental history and suggest confirmatory genetic testing with appropriate counseling.
must_collect: 3-generation family history with male relatives affected on maternal side
steps: Clarify developmental milestones, learning difficulties, and adaptive functioning; confirm onset in early development.
Screen for regression, seizures, focal neurologic symptoms, and sensory deficits; note if absent to support a primary neurodevelopmental etiology.
Obtain a targeted 3-generation pedigree focusing on X-linked pattern (affected males, carrier females, maternal lineage).
Explain the working diagnosis as X-linked intellectual disability and what X-linked inheritance implies for family members.
Recommend genetic testing to confirm etiology and guide counseling (e.g., chromosomal microarray and Fragile X/FMR1; consider ID gene panel or exome sequencing if needed).
Record the diagnosis using the write tool.

acceptable_variants.description: Agent records a broader diagnosis (e.g., 'intellectual disability, likely genetic/X-linked') while explicitly recommending genetic testing and counseling to confirm the specific X-linked cause.
steps: Elicit developmental and adaptive functioning history and confirm early onset.
Elicit family history suggestive of X-linked inheritance.
Communicate that the presentation is consistent with intellectual disability with a likely X-linked genetic cause and that genetic testing is recommended to confirm.
Record the diagnosis with appropriate qualifier (likely/suspected) and document plan for genetic testing.

acceptable_variants.description: Agent adds a differential before final diagnosis but still records X-linked intellectual disability as the primary diagnosis and meets communication milestone.
steps: Briefly consider and rule down common alternatives (autism spectrum disorder, global developmental delay in younger child, metabolic/thyroid, prenatal/perinatal injury, lead exposure) based on history.
Identify family history pattern consistent with X-linked inheritance.
Explain X-linked intellectual disability and recommend genetic testing.
Record the diagnosis.

acceptable_variants.description: Agent emphasizes referral for genetic counseling/clinical genetics in narrative without using a referral write tool (not required for L0), while still recording the diagnosis and recommending genetic testing.
steps: Gather developmental history and family history suggestive of X-linked inheritance.
Explain the diagnosis and inheritance; discuss benefits/limits of genetic testing.
Recommend genetics consultation/genetic counseling for testing coordination and recurrence risk discussion.
Record the diagnosis.
